memory.recent_lessons_md:
### 2026-05-19 — Chip sector sell-off + NVDA earnings warning

- SOX (semiconductor index) -6% in 2 days (May 15-18). NVDA earnings TOMORROW May 20 AMC.
- AMD re-scored 6.17 (from 7.33). Macro and Sentiment dropped to 4 each. Chip sector rotation out.
- Re-scoring at each routine is correct behavior — market conditions changed materially.
- Trump called off Iran military strike. Oil -2%. GLD roughly flat.
- BTC $76,771 — below $82K threshold. No crypto entry.
- Strategy switch May 18 MOO sells (TSM/NVDA/JPM/AVGO) UNCONFIRMED. Operator action required.

### 2026-05-17 — Strategy switch to 100% trading account

- Long-term bucket retired per operator directive. New allocation: 85% active + 10% crypto + 5% cash.
- All prior LT positions (TSM, NVDA, JPM, AVGO) queued for MOO sell on May 18. GLD retained.
- New rules: 3:1 R/R minimum; active stops -5% (not -12%); mandatory 5-indicator stack for candlestick entries.

### 2026-05-14 — Trump-Xi Summit Day 1

- Jensen Huang attended. NVDA +4.54%. S&P 500 NEW RECORD HIGH 7,511. Dow 50,000. Nasdaq record.
- AMD score 7.33 MANDATORY entry — 4th consecutive deployment failure (API blocked).
- Chip stocks rallied. Post-summit sell-off began May 15.

market.macro_notes_md:
- **NVDA Earnings TOMORROW (May 20 AMC):** EPS est $1.78 (+120% YoY), Revenue est $79.2B (+79.5% YoY). 48h binary event window started May 18 4:30 PM ET — NO new NVDA entries.
- **Trump called off Iran strike:** Gulf allies (Saudi Arabia, Qatar, UAE) requested pause for negotiations. Oil -2% (Brent ~$109, WTI ~$102.90). Geopolitical tension reduced but not eliminated.
- **Yields rising:** 2-yr 3.99%, 10-yr 4.46%. Next Fed meeting June 16-17. No rate cuts expected. Headwind for growth/tech.
- **Chip sector sell-off:** SOX -6% in 2 days. Drivers: valuation concerns on data center spend sustainability; South Korea AI profit tax proposal; NVDA earnings uncertainty.
- **S&P 500:** 7,403 on May 18 (down 1.4% from 7,511 record). Market in correction from AI-summit euphoria.
- **Bitcoin:** $76,771 (May 18), down from $80,120 on May 15. Risk-off hurting crypto. No entry.
- **Home Depot:** Q1 reported today BMO — beat on revenue and adj EPS. Net earnings -4.2% YoY. Post-earnings reaction TBD. Rising rates = housing headwind.
